title: quercus ilex var. expansa
intro: Quercus ilex var. expansa, also known as the holm oak or evergreen oak, is a variety of evergreen tree native to the Mediterranean region, characterized by its broad, rounded leaves and dense, spreading habit. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects, which have been attributed to the presence of various polyphenolic compounds. The health benefits of Quercus ilex var. expansa include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve cardiovascular health, and support immune function, as well as its use as an antidiarrheal and antiseptic agent. Herbal preparations of Quercus ilex var. expansa include tea, tinctures, and infusions, which are often used to promote digestion, alleviate respiratory issues, and support overall well-being.
